result.summary: Found several visual issues, mostly related to horizontal clipping/overflow and fixed navigation overlapping page content.

issues.description: Several horizontally arranged items are visibly cut off at the right edge, including the top category tab labeled “Hot Ga...” and multiple game cards in the game sections. This makes the page look horizontally overflowing or improperly sized for the viewport.
issues.suggestion: Ensure horizontal carousels are intentionally scrollable with clear affordance, or adjust card/tab widths and spacing so content fits cleanly within the viewport.

issues.description: The fixed bottom navigation overlaps the game grid content near the bottom of the screen, hiding parts of the lower game cards.
issues.suggestion: Add sufficient bottom padding or safe-area spacing to the page content so the final row can scroll fully above the fixed navigation bar.

issues.description: The “Hot Ga...” category label is truncated at the viewport edge, making the label incomplete and less readable.
issues.suggestion: Use shorter labels, allow proper horizontal scrolling, or ensure the active tab row has enough space so labels are not awkwardly clipped.

issues.description: The game sections show partial third-column cards on the right, which may look accidental if this is intended to be a grid rather than a carousel.
issues.suggestion: If these are carousels, consider adding a subtle scroll indicator or consistent peeking behavior. If they are grids, change the layout to two columns on this screen width.
